storyInfo: Elena successfully restores the abnormal readings observed the night before the Catastrophe, and discovers that an extraterrestrial object had fallen on Kjerag. She leaves the observation station to seek the truth, and to find the missing Carolin.

attributes.content: <p=1>Rhine Lab Observation Station, Kjerag</><p=2>December 7th, 1101</>
attributes.name: group_location_stamp

attributes.content: 33 hours before the Kjerag Catastrophe
Instruments, check. Terminal battery, check. Pen ink and paper, check... coffee, check!
attributes.name: Elena
Elena
attributes.content: It's all-nighter time—

attributes.name: Elena
attributes.content: Whoa!
A rustling sound coming from a corner gives Elena a fright. Only then does she realize that she is not the only person in the observation room.
A human figure crawls out of the pile of waste paper by the instruments.
Oh... it's you, Elena.
attributes.name: Carolin

attributes.content: (Yawns) I thought some great scientist was pulling an all-nighter in an effort to win the Max Prize...
attributes.name: Carolin

attributes.content: C-Carolin?
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: You remember me?
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Of course I remember the rising star of the Energy Section, the youngest researcher ever to join our ranks.
attributes.name: Carolin

attributes.content: I-I'm flattered...
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: I thought you had already left. The schedule said I was supposed to be the one on observation duty today—
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Heh, like the schedule's ever meant anything—I wouldn't have been sleeping here for two straight weeks if it did.
attributes.name: Carolin
Elena
attributes.content: No wonder I've hardly seen you ever since I came to the station...

attributes.name: Elena
attributes.content: Must be tough.
How does it feel to be back?
attributes.name: Carolin

attributes.content: You mean...?
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Coming back to Rhine Lab.
attributes.name: Carolin

attributes.content: You were the first person I thought of when they started recruiting for the observation station project, but I thought you would turn it down.
attributes.name: Carolin

attributes.content: Honestly, it wasn't an easy decision.
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: At the end of the day, though, the company gave me a valuable opportunity. Besides, the achievements of Rhine Lab itself have never been in question.
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: I still think my time at Rhine Lab was the closest I've ever been to achieving my dreams.
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: And I have to admit... even Ferdinand was a great researcher, if nothing else.
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Indeed. He has a mind for physics, so let him do what he does best.
attributes.name: Carolin

attributes.content: You know, he's stopped messing with other researchers since he got out of prison, and has been spending all his time on research.
attributes.name: Carolin

attributes.content: I think there's going to be a new Director of Energy before long... maybe she's standing in front of me now.
attributes.name: Carolin

attributes.content: No no no no no no, you've got to be kidding me!
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: I just wanted to get a closer look...
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Elena looks up and sees the sky obscured by snow through the observation's clear dome.
She hates this feeling.
Just like she hates her family's backwards traditions and beliefs, all the things in her way that have nothing to do with science itself.
She believes that science should be pure, ever-advancing, solving questions about Terra one at a time...
This observation station is an invaluable opportunity.
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: I only want to focus on my research, maybe push a little further than the boundaries of existing knowledge, if I can.
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Boundaries, huh... this is the closest place there is.
attributes.name: Carolin

attributes.content: Say, have you heard the legend of Kjerag?
attributes.name: Carolin

attributes.content: Legend? What legend?
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: This land has not suffered a Catastrophe for a thousand years, thanks to the protection of their god.
attributes.name: Carolin

attributes.content: Well, every country once attributed unusual phenomena to the 'gods'.
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Kjerag just held onto this tradition longer because of its isolation.
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Yet it is indeed true that Kjerag has not suffered a Catastrophe for a thousand years.
attributes.name: Carolin

attributes.content: It's certainly unusual... but there must be a reason.
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Maybe the high elevation makes it difficult for Originium particles to converge, or—
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: A Feranmut?
attributes.name: Carolin

attributes.content: Huh?
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Come on, I'm being serious!
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: I know there are legends about the Feranmuts, ancient beings the size of mountains that turn into mountains and rivers after death.
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: First off, there are no confirmed accounts proving the existence of Feranmuts. Besides, if these beings did exist, where did they find the energy to sustain their biological activities?
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Why bring this up, anyway?
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Just something that occurred to me while we were chatting.
attributes.name: Carolin

attributes.content: Well, that's enough gabbing. You can rest, I'll take over observations for the night.
attributes.name: Carolin

attributes.content: Really? But you've done a bunch of night shifts in a row—
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: It's not like my circadian rhythm can get any more messed up. And chatting with you woke me up.
attributes.name: Carolin

attributes.content: If I recall correctly, there are still some bugs to iron out with the power supply to the high-energy particle beam?
attributes.name: Carolin

attributes.content: Wow, y-you know about that?
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Get some rest and put that brain of yours to better use.
attributes.name: Carolin

attributes.content: Well, I guess I'll take you up on the offer! Thank you so much!
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: I'll buy you some fins and chips from that Victorian restaurant that just opened at the foot of the mountain when you have time!
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Please don't, no Victorian wants to have to enjoy authentic Victorian cuisine in a foreign land.
attributes.name: Carolin

attributes.content: Hehe... Kjerag cheese fondue, then?
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: That sounds great.
attributes.name: Carolin

attributes.content: It is another deep and quiet night in Kjerag. The ancient land slumbers, and lamps give way to stars.
The sleeping snowy mountains may seem wondrous to the uninitiated, though most who live on this land have gotten used to this sight.
Few look up at the sky on a night like this.
What's that...?!
attributes.name: Carolin

attributes.content: Both her eyes and the instruments capture the strange light that cut across the night sky.
Like a sign from the heavens.
It appears that Carolin was an accomplished, intelligent young researcher with a bright future.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: This project is very important to both Rhine Lab and Columbia. The recruiting process was extremely selective.
attributes.name: Silence

attributes.content: We have yet to find any irregularities in her records and communication logs.
attributes.name: Silence

attributes.content: She was originally from Victoria?
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: I am unable to provide any private information. That would be a violation of Rhine Lab's privacy policy, and basic human rights.
attributes.name: Silence

attributes.content: We can only provide information about her time at Rhine Lab, unless your government officially presses charges of espionage.
attributes.name: Silence

attributes.content: Of course. I was just asking.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: Her application material was very comprehensive, with every shift and fieldwork experience documented meticulously...
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: Here, it seems she even requested a field survey task for herself.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: Collecting information about Kjerag's geography concurrently with her scientific research?
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: She has been a diligent researcher, highly rated at Rhine Lab, and a model student when she was at school.
attributes.name: Silence

attributes.content: Such scientists are not usually involved in the kind of trouble that we currently face.
attributes.name: Silence

attributes.content: Yet, at this point, we have no information or clue as to the whereabouts of Carolin.
attributes.name: Silence

attributes.content: Believe it or not, we are even more eager to find Carolin than you are.
attributes.name: Silence

attributes.content: There are many things at stake. Rhine Lab's reputation, the security of the research data, potential breach of our management system, the mutual trust and partnership between us, and...
attributes.name: Silence

attributes.content: And?
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: ...And Kjerag's development.
attributes.name: Silence

attributes.content: Are you speaking as a member of the Scientific Ethics Consensus Committee?
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: Please don't misunderstand. Though it might sound a little condescending, I have the highest respect for what Karlan Trade is doing.
attributes.name: Silence

attributes.content: After all, the courage to reach beyond one's comfort zone is the most important quality in a scientist.
attributes.name: Silence

attributes.content: Not just in scientists.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: There is a saying in Kjerag, 'Black burdenbeasts are bad burdenbeasts.'
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: They jump their fences and run into the snowfields. Herders who go into the fields following their tracks never return.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: They die?
attributes.name: Silence

attributes.content: That's what 'go into the snowfields and never return' usually means in Kjerag.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: One by one, pages are reviewed and placed on the table—Carolin's notes and actions turn into a tiny pile of papers.
But where did the black burdenbeasts go? It's the same question, in the end.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: Perhaps they led the herders into the mountains, getting lost in the forests and freezing to death at night.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: Another page is read and placed on the table.
Or perhaps...
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: Gnosis puts the last page down.
White upon white, like snowflakes falling on frozen cliffs.
They simply found a new source of water, a new haven.
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: Can you at least tell me where exactly she carried out her fieldwork?
attributes.name: Gnosis

attributes.content: I can't. The observation data are badly corrupted. I can't recover the original data from the port...
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Stacks of documents and mugs with dried coffee stains sit quietly on the table.
An error tone signifies failure of the data restoration, causing their hearts to sink once more.
There are ID records of all entries and exits, no message left behind in the dorms or labs...
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: The main security camera malfunctioned, while the two to the sides of the lab didn't capture anything...
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: It seems like too much of a coincidence. Could Carolin really have...?
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: No. Everyone else might assume she's a spy, but I can't let bias cloud my judgment. I already requested access permissions from Director Muelsyse to get to the bottom of things...
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Knock, knock. Who's been up all night?
attributes.name: Muelsyse

attributes.content: D-Director Muelsyse?
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Surprised to see me?
attributes.name: Muelsyse

attributes.content: I hear you haven't been back to the dorms for two straight nights... are you still searching for the data that disappeared?
attributes.name: Muelsyse

attributes.content: Yes...
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: There is little doubt that the observation logs were altered, although we're not sure who did it, or why.
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: There is reason to believe that the overwritten data hold a crucial clue...
attributes.name: Elena
Muelsyse
attributes.content: Hm... but...

attributes.name: Muelsyse
attributes.content: Never mind. I brought you a li'l snacky. Can't work on an empty stomach, after all.
Thank you. What is this?
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: You've never had it? Kjerag cheese pastries with sugar frosting and berry jam. It was all the rage at the station a while back.
attributes.name: Muelsyse

attributes.content: I heard people talking about it and had planned on trying them out the next time I went down the mountain for fondue, but I've been too busy investigating special energy sources unique to Kjerag.
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Elena picks up a pastry. It's dried up on the outside, but the cheese and jam inside remain moist after being heated.
The sound of running water ceases, and Muelsyse sits down by her side with two clean mugs in hand.
Mind if I sip a cuppa joe with you?
attributes.name: Muelsyse

attributes.content: Not at all.
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Fresh coffee fills the mugs, drenching the air in its aroma.
The sound of machines operating is clearly audible at this late hour. A massive window on the wall displays the stars in Kjerag's night sky, deep and boundless as always.
The atmosphere in the room relaxes. Elena lifts her mug after a moment of hesitation.
Midnight coffee in the lab. Brings back memories.
attributes.name: Muelsyse

attributes.content: You used to stay up late too?
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Who doesn't, in our line of work?
attributes.name: Muelsyse

attributes.content: Even when I was a student, it felt like I had more coffee than blood in veins for finals—
attributes.name: Muelsyse

attributes.content: Haha... Looks like everyone's been there before.
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Working at night's more efficient, after all.
attributes.name: Muelsyse

attributes.content: Yes. Night is quiet. Makes it easier to focus.
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: I used to write one paper while dictating the summary of another using an audio recorder, when I was really busy.
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: That way, I get to make progress on both at the same time. Crazy, when I think back to it...
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Oh yeah! Audio recorder!
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Huh? Audio? Where are you going?
attributes.name: Muelsyse

attributes.content: Surveillance audio recordings!
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Surveillance camera, credentials... the time is... 3:19 am...
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: There.
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Didn't you say the cameras didn't capture what was happening at the lab table?
attributes.name: Muelsyse

attributes.content: Yes... but it might have captured sound from the equipment.
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Listen to this... a lot of instruments operating. This ticking sound should be the audio sensor.
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: The frequency that it plays back is directly connected to graphing. We can use this frequency to recreate—excuse me.
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Need a pen and paper? Here you go.
attributes.name: Muelsyse

attributes.content: Oh, thanks.
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Pen tip rubs against paper. The time they spend around the instruments have made researchers intimately familiar with data.
She enters the numbers that she copied down into the computer, then...
There...
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: This is the hidden data.
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Lemme take a look-see...
attributes.name: Muelsyse

attributes.content: Looks like a small earthquake occurred on Mount Karlan the morning of the day before the Catastrophe.
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: No... not an earthquake. It was an impact of some kind.
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: As if something fell from the sky into the mountains of Kjerag.
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: And then... came Kjerag's first Catastrophe.
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Outside the wide windows of the observation station, the mountains of Kjerag seem to reflect a translucent blue under the moon.
Did Carolin go looking for this extraterrestrial object?
attributes.name: Muelsyse

attributes.content: But why would she do it all on her own?
attributes.name: Muelsyse

attributes.content: She's never abandoned her post like this. She must have found something...
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: We might be able to find her, if we figure out where that thing fell!
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Which we can calculate from the wave frequency of the sound of impact!
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: Director Muelsyse, we...
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: The Vine-Bead Court isn't about to investigate on our behalf.
attributes.name: Muelsyse

attributes.content: Strictly speaking, we shouldn't get any more involved in any of this stuff. The most we can do is to report this to Component Control.
attributes.name: Muelsyse

attributes.content: But Carolin might be in danger...
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: You know that's not your job.
attributes.name: Muelsyse

attributes.content: You're not exactly close to Carolin. Bluntly, whatever happens to her is none of your business.
attributes.name: Muelsyse

attributes.content: Muelsyse finishes her cold coffee in silence.
Frowning at the bitter taste, she takes a piece of pastry and puts it in her mouth, speaking as she chews.
Sometimes, I kinda think the most important quality in a scientist is courage... maybe even recklessness.
attributes.name: Muelsyse

attributes.content: You're a sharp kiddo. Got a knack for finding rhythm in music.
attributes.name: Muelsyse

attributes.content: Makes for a good dancer. I wouldn't wanna tie you up, gotta let you move around free.
attributes.name: Muelsyse

attributes.content: You mean...?
attributes.name: Elena

attributes.content: This conversation never happened. Nobody tried to talk you out of it, nobody told you what you can or can't do...
attributes.name: Muelsyse

attributes.content: As for this late night snack... you took it from the shared fridge yourself, didn't you?
attributes.name: Muelsyse
